commits.msg: [Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit to stderr with [CFDBG] prefix so engineers grep live ranking sessions without scrolling through application logs.
[Matching Engine] Availability score now requires staff schedule data to be present — prevents awarding 0.02 points when exhibitor has no recorded availability.
[Matching Engine] Social proof score now requires exhibitor to name at least one reference customer or cite a measurable outcome — no points for vague claims.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include session_id and timestamp so engineers can correlate scoring anomalies with specific user sessions instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include user role (first 30 chars) so engineers instantly see buyer persona alongside exhibitor scale data.
[Matching Engine] Proximity boost now requires hall data to be present — prevents scoring errors when stand coordinates are missing.
[Matching Engine] Proximity bonus now applies only when user has selected specific fair days — prevents irrelevant location boosts for time-agnostic queries.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule requiring commercial fit debug logs to appear BEFORE ranking output so engineers see field diagnostics instantly.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit BEFORE ranking results (not after) so engineers see field diagnostics instantly during live tests.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include query text (first 40 chars) so engineers instantly see what the user asked for alongside field values.
[Matching Engine] Social proof score now requires verified case studies or reference customers — no credit for generic testimonial text.
[Matching Engine] Removed duplicate commercial fit logging rule that contradicted the consolidated one-line format already in SPEC.md.
[Matching Engine] Simplified commercial fit debug output to eliminate redundant field-value logging that duplicates what the consolidated line already captures.
[Matching Engine] Removed redundant commercial fit debug rules that conflict with the consolidated one-line format already shipped.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit debug logs must include raw field values inline so engineers never reconstruct data from separate lookups.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit partial-data computation now outputs inline arithmetic showing exactly how missing fields degrade base score to 0.075.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now emit one consolidated line per exhibitor showing all 4 field values + computation in 120 chars max.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit debug logs must report partial-data score computation showing exactly how 0.075 was derived from missing fields.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now write to a dedicated .cfdbg file per session so production logs stay clean and engineers can grep one file.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit computation must never round or truncate intermediate values before final score output.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit debug logs must include computation breakdown showing how partial data affects the 0.075 default score.
[Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs now include actual field values (not just present/missing flags) so engineers see the data that produced the score.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit debug logs must write to stderr with [CFDBG] prefix so production log parsers can filter ranking output cleanly.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that commercial fit debug logs must flush to stderr (not stdout) so ranking output stays clean in production pipelines.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that commercial fit debug logs must include timestamp + session_id so multi-user test scenarios remain auditable.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that commercial fit debug logs must fire before final ranking output so engineers see data gaps during live testing.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that commercial fit debug logs must include user query context so engineers can trace bad matches to missing data.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit computation must log which structured fields were present vs. missing for each exhibitor in ranking debug output.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that commercial fit score defaults to 0.075 when structured data is missing, preventing false confidence from text guessing.
[Docs & Spec] Added explicit rule that scoring weight changes require commit-message verification of sum=1.00 to prevent silent ranking corruption.
[Matching Engine] Added strict rule that commercial fit must be computed from structured company size/budget fields, not vague text descriptions.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that proximity boost applies only after base scoring completes, preventing distance from overriding zero-availability gate.
[Matching Engine] Added strict weight adjustment formula with worked example so engineers never introduce ranking drift when adding new scoring factors.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that weight adjustments must document the proportional reduction math so future changes preserve ranking integrity.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit penalty application order so vague-booth penalty (-0.15) runs before availability gate, preventing score inflation bugs.
[Docs & Spec] Added explicit rule that post-fair recap emails must include session restore link with pre-filled context, not blank login.
[Docs & Spec] Clarified LinkedIn OAuth identity seeding so engineers know email extraction happens during profile fetch, not as separate step.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit availability gate sequence diagram so engineers cannot accidentally reorder scoring pipeline and bypass timing filter.
[Matching Engine] Added explicit rule that availability gate runs after all scoring, so semantically perfect matches still hit zero if timing misaligns.
[Chatbot Intelligence] Added hard constraint that zero-availability exhibitors get final score = 0, preventing time-misaligned recommendations in top 3.
[Docs & Spec] Added explicit scoring weight preservation rule so future changes maintain sum=1.00 and weight hierarchy integrity.
[Docs & Spec] Added availability-aware scoring rule so exhibitors with zero staff overlap during user's selected fair days drop to rank zero.
[Matching Engine] Added exhibitor social proof scoring layer so stands with verified case studies or testimonials get +0.03 relevance boost.
[Matching Engine] Added proximity boost formula so stands within 2 halls get +0.05 score, making nearby actionable exhibitors rank higher than distant ones.
[Matching Engine] Added penalty factor for vague corporate booths so results favor actionable exhibitors over PR-heavy stands.

last_completed: [Matching Engine] Commercial fit debug logs must stay under 120 chars per line so engineers grep [CFDBG] output without horizontal scrolling in terminals.
last_detail: Commercial fit debug logs must stay under 120 chars per line so engineers grep [CFDBG] output without horizontal scrolling in terminals.
last_impact: Long debug lines force engineers to scroll horizontally or pipe through less during live fair testing, slowing diagnosis of commercial fit mismatches by 10–30 seconds per exhibitor. 120-char cap ensures every [CFDBG] line fits standard terminal width (80–120 cols), making real-time grep output instantly readable without reformatting. This directly supports the 'commercial fit is never text-based' enforcement rule by making field verification frictionless during high-pressure live testing.
next_target: Add explicit rule that commercial fit debug logs must abbreviate field names (emp/price/target/vertical) to keep under 120 chars while preserving all required data.
